proposedPrinciple: Classify feedback before assigning agent work. Production corrections, user edits, or review comments should become agent tasks only after evidence-backed triage separates actionable repeated failures from preferences, unsupported behavior, ambiguous signals, and expected workflow noise.
durabilityReason: The lesson is vendor-neutral and applies to any self-improving agent system that uses production feedback or human corrections to create future work.
additiveRationale: Existing Foundation principles require evidence-backed promotion and reviewable change flow; this adds the missing pre-promotion classification gate that prevents noisy production feedback from becoming unsafe agent work.
